description: Config SSOT for edu/lora/hexad_balanced_sampler.hexa (cluster #31 improvement 7). Deterministic round-robin sampler over the 6 Hexad axes {c,d,e,m,s,w}.

rules_doc: 1) Input line may carry an explicit tag as a leading token of the form '<axis>	<text>' or '<axis>|<text>' where axis in {c,d,e,m,s,w}. If present it overrides keyword inference.
2) Otherwise we lowercase the line and test each axis keyword list in priority_order; first axis whose keyword list matches wins.
3) If no keyword matches, the line is tagged '?' (unknown) and is NOT admitted to any axis bucket.
4) Keyword lists are copied from tool/corpus_generator.hexa HEXAD_*_KW (commit 1813648e lineage) to keep tagger SSOT aligned with the corpus generator.

description: Round-robin over axes in priority_order. At each step we pick the next-in-rotation axis and take its next unused line (in original order).
When an axis bucket is exhausted, we 'recycle' from its start (deterministic, reproducible with seed). Recycle continues until batch_size is reached.
Axes whose bucket is empty are skipped with a single WARN and do not block batch assembly. Per-axis count difference must be <= epsilon (measured on non-empty axes).

raw: raw#10 proof-carrying: selftest result JSON + verdict persisted
raw#15 no hard-coded answers — keywords & thresholds live in this config
